reasoning: The Turks and Caicos Islands have a very small population of around 45,000 and rarely experience civil unrest. According to the background information, there were exactly zero protests in the 360 days preceding the forecast date, establishing a baseline of 1.000 (after adding one). To resolve positive, there would need to be strictly more than 10 protests (i.e., 11+ distinct protest events) recorded by ACLED within a 30-day window for each resolution date. Searching local news from November 2025 reveals decreasing crime trends and no ongoing protests or escalating tensions that would lead to unprecedented massive unrest. Given that 11 protest events in 30 days would require near-continuous daily demonstrations in a tiny territory, the probabilities are exceptionally low. I have set a near-floor probability of 0.02 for the first two near-term dates, and minimally increased it to 0.04 for the furthest date to account for standard time-horizon uncertainty.
